title: Beet root — heavy basal + a single top-dressing for root bulking
body: HORDI runs beet root on a notably front-loaded fertilizer schedule: basal Urea 165 + TSP 270 + MOP 125 kg/ha plus 10 t/ha organic manure, then a single top-dressing at week 3 (Urea 165 + MOP 125). This is heavier than most root crops HORDI publishes, because beet's 75–90 day root-bulking window needs sustained N+K to reach the 12–20 t/ha target yield. Crimson Globe and Detroit Dark Red are the two released varieties — both work in all AEZs but up-country cool climate gives the best pigment development (deeper red hypocotyl). Soil pH 6.3–7.3 is tighter than most HORDI crops; below 6.3 the roots go corky, above 7.3 boron becomes unavailable and hollow-heart develops. Test soil pH before a commercial 5–6 kg/ha planting.
quote: Basal: Urea 165, TSP 270, MOP 125. Top dressing (3 weeks post-planting): Urea 165, MOP 125. Organic manure requirement: 10 t/ha. pH 6.3-7.3. Harvest: 75-90 days after transplanting. Yield: 12-20 t/ha.
